items.title: OpenAI and Nvidia CEOs didn’t flinch at Trump’s $100,000 H-1B visa fee, and now they’re paying up as their application numbers soar
items.summary: While frontier AI companies boost their number of foreign workers, other tech giants with much larger workforces are cutting back.

items.title: BYD threatens to sue Trump administration over Pentagon military list
items.summary:
BYD is threatening to sue the US government after the Pentagon added the world’s largest EV maker to its list of “Chinese military companies” this week.



Stella Li, BYD’s executive vice-president, said the company will use every legal “weapon” at its dispos…

items.title: Companies' new budget-friendly approach to AI could create a corporate caste system
items.summary: Companies want to cut down on their AI spending. Doing so means making some hard choices that risk creating internal issues.

items.title: Crazy Taxi is back, but Sega's use of generative AI steals the spotlight
items.summary: Revealed during an Xbox showcase, Crazy Taxi: World Tour is slated for release in 2027 on Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, Windows PC, and Xbox Series X. The footage shows a fast-paced drive through a bright, San Francisco-style city, with the original drive…

items.title: A German court says Google’s AI Overviews are Google’s own words, and it’s liable when they’re false
items.summary: A German court has ruled that Google is directly liable for false claims its AI Overviews make, treating the AI-written summaries as Google’s own speech rather than ordinary search results. It is one of the first rulings to test who is responsible when a gene…

items.title: Decart's new world model can simulate hours of photorealistic driving — with some caveats
items.summary: Decart is launching Oasis 3, a real-time world model that generates photorealistic driving environments for autonomous vehicle testing, now available via API for developers to build on.

items.title: Poetic emerges from stealth with $50M from OpenAI to automate insurance underwriting and compliance
items.summary: Poetic, an AI startup that has been operating in stealth, has emerged with $50M in funding and a $500M valuation to automate some of finance’s most sensitive back-office work, from insurance underwriting to compliance and fraud checks. Its backers include Ope…

items.title: Why everyone’s an energy company now
items.summary: Electricity demand from AI data centers is pushing everyone — including automakers like GM and Ford — into the energy storage business.

items.title: Meet the CMO trying to make nuclear fusion cool
items.summary: Commonwealth Fusion Systems is ramping up outreach to creators as it looks to raise awareness of nuclear fusion among Gen Z and Gen Alpha.

items.title: Why two SpaceX alumni are betting on solar and batteries to power the AI craze
items.summary: Ambrosia Energy wants to build power plants in less than 12 months while undercutting natural gas. It hopes to build gigawatts worth by 2030.

items.title: Signal attacks UK plan to scan devices for nude images as "mass surveillance"
items.summary: The device-scanning proposal was announced by Prime Minister Keir Starmer at London Tech Week. Under the plan, companies such as Apple and Google would be expected to activate built-in features on smartphones and tablets that detect and block nude images on c…

items.title: Capsa AI raises $18M to build the ‘AI operating system’ for private equity
items.summary: Capsa AI, a London and New York startup building what it calls an “AI operating system” for private capital, has raised $18M in Series A funding. The round was co-led by TX Ventures and Pivot Investment Partners, with participation from Bek Ventures, and take…

items.title: U.S. strikes Iran, SpaceX's unique IPO, Kalshi's insider trading fight and more in Morning Squawk
items.summary: Here are five key things investors need to know to start the trading day.

items.title: ‘The circulatory system isn’t working.’ Goldman on what’s really wrong with private markets
items.summary: Goldman Sachs' top private markets executives say the industry is at a structural inflection point — not a crisis. Not everyone agrees.

items.title: Geico's famous gecko is getting an AI makeover
items.summary: Geico is using generative AI to enable its gecko mascot to give podcast interviews and react to live events.
